data.narrative:
Xage Security is a Series B defense and security company founded by Kamesh Raghavendra, Susanto Irwan, and Roman Arutyunov, having raised $80M to date. The peer universe comprises 8 private companies and 2 public comparables in the Defense & Security sector.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $80M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implying $152M. Peer-median analysis examines 8 same-sector, same-stage private companies—including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, and Castelion—which span $300M to $1.5B with a median valuation of $1.1B.

The triangulated analysis yields a low estimate of $129M, a median of $638M, and a high of $1.3B. The wide spread reflects a substantial gap between stage-progression mechanics (suggesting $152M) and peer comps (median $1.1B), indicating sensitivity to assumptions about comparable company relevance and capital efficiency relative to peers. Key uncertainties include revenue/margin trajectory relative to the peer set, customer concentration, and whether stage-progression or peer-median better reflects market conditions for Series B defense-tech fundraising. A valuation in the $400M–$900M range may warrant closer examination of unit economics and customer retention relative to cited peers.
